characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: 10-Minute Retirement: End of season one/early season two, resigns shortly after Dino Rossi’s arrest for Terry Coulis’ murder, as he’s fallen in love with Dino’s sister Christina Brock. He later rejoins the unit a month after Christina’s death at the hands of Dino, after helping Mac track down the culprit blackmailing them both about their relationship. Backstory: Was a uniformed police officer and engaged to a fellow police officer named Alice, until she was shot and killed by a lone gunman, later revealed to beAaron Fielder. This causes Church to quit the police force and live off the grid for several years, until Bernie Rocca convinces him to return to the police force as part of his Undercover unit. Deadpan Snarker: Has his moments. Cool Car: For the first few seasons of the show, Church drives a late-90s Subaru Impreza WRX. However, from season six until the end of the series, Church drives a early-2000s Holden Monaro. Da Chief: Church is briefly the acting boss of the Undercover unit at the start of season six, following Mac’s departure at the end of the previous season, before the unit is soon permutated into Special Investigations under the command of Luke Harris. At the end of the series, Church becomes the acting boss of the unit once again (along with Special Investigations) after Harrisgets himself suspended for attacking Angie’s fiancé Sean Hunter.

characters.name: Peter Church
characters.description: 10-Minute Retirement: End of season one/early season two, resigns shortly after Dino Rossi’s arrest for Terry Coulis’ murder, as he’s fallen in love with Dino’s sister Christina Brock. He later rejoins the unit a month after Christina’s death at the hands of Dino, after helping Mac track down the culprit blackmailing them both about their relationship. Backstory: Was a uniformed police officer and engaged to a fellow police officer named Alice, until she was shot and killed by a lone gunman, later revealed to beAaron Fielder. This causes Church to quit the police force and live off the grid for several years, until Bernie Rocca convinces him to return to the police force as part of his Undercover unit. Deadpan Snarker: Has his moments. Cool Car: For the first few seasons of the show, Church drives a late-90s Subaru Impreza WRX. However, from season six until the end of the series, Church drives a early-2000s Holden Monaro. Da Chief: Church is briefly the acting boss of the Undercover unit at the start of season six, following Mac’s departure at the end of the previous season, before the unit is soon permutated into Special Investigations under the command of Luke Harris. At the end of the series, Church becomes the acting boss of the unit once again (along with Special Investigations) after Harrisgets himself suspended for attacking Angie’s fiancé Sean Hunter.

characters.name: Angie Piper
characters.description: Action Girl: Throughout the series. Action Mom: Angie becomes this in season seven, shortly after giving birth to her son Josh. In season eight, she rescues Josh after he is taken by a group of kidnappers operating a child kidnapping ring, and Angie kills one of the kidnappers in self-defense. Amicable Exes: Angie breaks up with Harris shortly after the incident withBrett Lintonin season seven that nearly got Angie killed, which she was somewhat hostile towards Harris over until they both discover that she is pregnant. Shortly after the birth of their son, Angie convinces Harris to allow her to return to Undercover following a stint working in Homicide, with the two being able to act as cordial co-workers to each other, up until near the end of the series, when Harris suspects Angie’s psychiatrist-turned-boyfriend Sean Hunter ofbeing a serial killer, leading to Angie becoming hostile towards Harris once again. Anti-Hero: Angie is on the side of good, but she is not without her faults: Lying to her superiors, doing borderline illegal things, and evenhelping her sister Sam, who was working as a drug mule in season five, flee the country to prevent her from getting arrested. Badass in Distress: She was kidnapped by Harris’ nemesis Brett Linton in the second half of season seven, and leaves such an impact on her that leads to her and Harris breaking up.

characters.name: Christina Dichiera
characters.description: Backstory: She was kicked out of home by her dad Phillip Mathews when she was 15, after which she became a street kid and a junkie, as well as later becoming a juvenile criminal, before deciding to change her life around and become a police officer. Badass in Distress: She was almost sexually assaulted by her undercover target Ivan Flitcroft after she seduced him into confessing to a murder, and is soon kidnapped and beaten by him when he is released, resulting in Chris being hospitalized. Deadpan Snarker: Has a habit of giving snarky remarks at times, especially towards Leo and Church. Dude Magnet: Just ask Leo, who thinks that she’s aFair Cop. Fair Cop: She’s absolutely hot and is fancied by many men and women she meets.

characters.name: Luke Harris
characters.description: Badass in a Nice Suit: 99% of his screen-time has him wearing an awesome suit. The Casanova: He's a major womanizer whoReally Gets Around, even while he’s in a serious relationship with Angie, which Harris blames on his bipolar disorder. Da Chief: Takes over as boss of the Undercover unit in season six, and remains so until the end of the series. However, in the series finale, hegets himself suspended for attacking Angie’s fiancé Sean Hunter.

characters.name: Ellen ‘Mac’MacKenzie
characters.description: Boyish Short Hair: From season four onwards. Da Chief: Takes over as boss of the Undercover unit in season three, and remains so until the end of season five, when she quits the police force and sails away with an old boyfriend. However, the rest of her unit (with the exception of Church) are unaware of her departure until the season six premiere. Deadpan Snarker: Has her moments. Luke, You Are My Father: This rare female version of the trope occurs late in season four, when Mac finds her birth mother Eve Reisner, and tells her that she is her daughter. Seems like Eve was pregnant with Mac after a one-night-stand as a teenager, and nearly had her aborted, although she later decided against it and instead gave Mac up for adoption shortly after her birth. Mac later saves Eve from murderous robber Roddy Green. Rank Up: Mac gradually gets promoted from Detective Sergeant to Detective Senior Sergeant in the season three premiere.

characters.name: Oscar Stone
characters.description: Driven to Suicide: Subverted.He prepares to shoot himself in the head after his cancer becomes terminal. However, he decides at the last minute not to go along with it. Heroic Sacrifice:An assassin disguised as an orderly, tasked with the hit on Angie’s fiancé Marty Engle, tries to shoot Church. Stone prevents this from happening by getting killed in his place. Lethal Diagnosis: Stone finds out in the second half of season five that he has testicular cancer, and despite having surgery, later becomes terminal. Took a Level in Jerkass: During the first half of season four, as it’s revealed that he’s using anabolic steroids to aid in his recovery after getting shot the first time at the end of season three. This ultimately transforms him into an aggressive douchebag and soon alienates himself from the rest of the unit, especially Angie. Although he soon reverts back to his friendly, nerdier self when he stopped taking steroids halfway through the season.

characters.name: Bernie Rocca
characters.description: Put on the Bus: Retires from the police force at the end of season two to spend time with his family shortly after getting shot and later recovering well enough in time to save the unit from being shut down.He later returns in season sevento help Church take down a dodgy stockbroker, while exhibiting signs that heTook a Level in Jerkass. Suddenly Bilingual: Early in season two, it is revealed that Rocca can speak Spanish, and in an attempt to prove to Church that it’s not rusty, he calls up a Spanish restaurant to order some food while speaking the language… only for the restaurant server on the phone to suddenlySwitch to Englishand say “Why do you Italians always think you can speak Spanish?”, before following up with “Stick to English, your accent’s terrible”, much to Rocca’s offense and Church’s amusement.

characters.name: Kaye Kelso
characters.description: Put on a Bus: Leaves after five episodes into season one, aftergetting abducted by a serial killer, and is later found mutilated but alive by Angie. Rocca later tells the unit that Kaye will recover, but will not be returning to unit.

characters.name: Danni Mayo
characters.description: Brutal Honesty: She never minces words and frequently tells exactly what's on her mind. Put on a Bus: Leaves early in season six, resigns from the police force after clashing with Harris, which initially led to her getting fired by him, although he later reversed this decision. Welcome Episode: Danni’s arrival into the Undercover unit is the focus of the season three premiere.

characters.name: Andrew Bligh
characters.description: Internal Affairs: Usually shows up working for Ethical Standards Department. Inspector Javert: Initially is hellbent on taking down Mac and the Undercover unit, but eventually abandons his crusade against Mac and later teams up with her, Angie and the rest of the unit to take downDirty CopHarry Love, who had Blackmailed him in the past.

characters.name: Christina Brock
characters.description: Character Death:Dies in Church's arms afterTaking the Bulletfor him by her brother Dino Rossi.

characters.name: Lauren Davies
characters.description: Ms. Fanservice: Lauren goes topless before she gets into Stone’s bed as he’s sleeping, and wakes him up to have sex with him.

characters.name: Marty Engle
characters.description: Bittersweet Ending: Marty is forced by Inspector Frank Callahan to enterWitness Protectionto testify against Nigel Finster following the latter’s arrest, leaving Angie heartbroken. He later survives an assassination attempt on him with the help of Angie, Church and Stone, but this ultimately results in Stone’s death. The Informant: He is initially coerced into becoming Angie’s informant in season five, after Angie threatens to have him arrested. He eventually becomes more willing to help Angie after they fall in love with each other.

characters.name: Megan Walsh
characters.description: Sixth Ranger Traitor: Joins the Undercover unit at the recommendation of Church and Harris, who is later revealed to be an extremelyDirty Cop.
